title: Nod Merchant-Voice Extension v0.1
description: Free-text narrative fields a merchant uses to express positioning, story, and specialties. Distinct from structured business metadata. Lives at extensions["ai.opennod.merchant-voice"] in a manifest. Agents may surface these to humans verbatim or use them to inform recommendations; protocol does not constrain interpretation.

positioning_statement.description: One-sentence statement of how the merchant positions itself. Length-capped to enforce concision; longer statements belong in 'story'.

ideal_customer.description: Plain-language description of the customer the merchant is built for. Used by agents during recommendation tasks; merchants should write the customer they actually serve, not an aspirational one.

story.description: Longer-form narrative: founding, what makes this merchant different, why it exists. May be used by agents for human-facing summaries.

specialties.description: Things this merchant is unusually good at, as short noun phrases. Examples: 'wood-fired Neapolitan pizza', 'IRS audit defense for small businesses'. Avoid generic claims that any competitor would also list.

accommodations.description: Customer accommodations the merchant explicitly offers, as short noun phrases. Examples: 'wheelchair-accessible entrance and restroom', 'gluten-free kitchen', 'after-hours appointments by request', 'on-site interpreter for ASL'. Use for capabilities a customer might filter on; not a marketing list.
